message:
fix(vdbe): correct INTEGER PRIMARY KEY column alignment for SQLite-created rows

When reading rows written by real SQLite (which stores the IPK value in
the record payload), the VDBE Column opcode and connection-level row
builder incorrectly shifted column indices, causing columns after the IPK
to appear in wrong positions. Introduce record_column_count() for fast
payload column counting, thread table_column_count_by_root_page through
to the engine, and apply a 3-tier heuristic to detect whether the payload
includes the rowid alias before deciding whether to shift.

Add compat test reading rusqlite-created AUTOINCREMENT tables, pager-backed
oracle probes for bd-rjc with BtCursor payload reads before and after
connection close, a sequential single-connection variant (640 iterations),
and a reopened AUTOINCREMENT WHERE+ORDER BY column-position test. Wire up
tracing for publication visibility tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
